info.topictitle: Japanese domestic issues: political and economic issues, and their impact on foreign relations
info.parenttitle: Japanese expansion in East Asia - Causes of expansion

markdown:
## **Japanese Domestic Issues: Political and Economic issues, and their Impact on Foreign Relations**


1. Even when Japan was already implementing an aggressive foreign policy, it **became much more expansionist from 1931 onwards.**
2. This is because of two main developments, the **growing tension between authoritarianism and democracy and the impact of the Great Depression.**

<callout type="tip">* Even** though the official reason that Japan will always present to legitimize their expansion **will be based on **the need to protect Japanese interests and self defense**
* The** real motives are more connected to their domestic politics and the Great Depression.**</callout>

<callout type="exam_technique">* Create a **table** with** evidence for supporting the argument of domestic policies **and the **one of the impact of the Great Depression to explain the expansion in the 1930s. **
* This will come in handy when you need to **build the essay in Paper 1.**</callout>

## **Authoritarianism Grows in Japan**


1. Even though the **Meiji constitution had brought some democracy through votes, in practice the Japanese government never represented true democracy.** **Cabinets** were set up to **protect the interests of the zaibatsu.**
2. This trend only got more pronounced when after **1929 the Great Depression impacted Japan** and widened the **gap between the wealthy zaibatsu and small businesses.**

****

![Meiji Constitution ](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/0bba973949a271e7de16920499a24b04128d2fe5-1600x1072.jpg)

## **The Great Depression in Japan**


1. **The Wall Street Crash** triggered a **severe economic crisis** in Japan:
  1. With **exports falling nearly 50% between 1929-1931**
  2. **Unemployment rising to 2.6 million by 1930**
  3. **Silk prices dropping** to just 20% of their 1923 value, which devastated farmers who heavily relied on silk production.
2. Japan went into a** recession that began with a financial crisis** that saw 25% of **banks collapse**. 
3. **Rapid urbanization and a population boom** **(42 million in 1895 to 70 million by 1933) strained resources**, while** rural incomes dropped by two-thirds from 1926 to 1931,** fueling **economic and social unrest.**

****

![The Great Depression in Japan](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/5bc4ed28c4cb952f8c378bada1832d27612777b2-1024x819.jpg)

<callout type="note">* **Economic hardship and public discontent** led many **Japanese to reject Western influence, democracy, and individualism. **
* **Rising nationalism**, especially in rural areas and the military, fueled **support for authoritarianism and militarized policies. **
* The **1930 London Naval Conference intensified tensions, **and **Prime Minister Hamaguchi was assassinated,** symbolizing **Japan’s shift toward collective strength **and an increasingly **aggressive national stance.**</callout>

<callout type="tip">* As you can see, there is a** common impact of the Great Depression in many countries: the disillusionment with democracy and the rise of authoritarianism.**
* There is a **correlation that can be traced between unemployment and general decay in life quality** and the **growing support for authoritarian and personalistic regimes.**</callout>
